news.title_en: Beyond the Feed: Sorcery's Fan Magazine in Print
news.content_en: Creativity, mastery, and wonder don't just live in the cards of Sorcery: Contested Realm. They live in the stories we tell about them, and somewhere within these moments, The Ruby Core-ier found its place as Sorcery's premier (and only) fan-made print magazine. The idea didn't begin with a grand plan. It started, as many Sorcery stories do, with the community. After a weekend at the Courtesan Cup in 2024, Jack/@ElektrickSorcerer came home energized. The event was the largest community gathering of its time, and something about it lit a fire in him. He wanted to contribute, to make something that celebrated the game and the people who played it. A blog felt too easy. A podcast was familiar territory, but not quite right. Whatever he built needed to feel old-school. It needed weight. It had to be something in physical print. If the game was going to have a print fanzine, it deserved a name worth carrying. Jack wanted something with a newspaper quality to it, something that felt like it belonged on a masthead. He turned the options over in his head until one landed cleanly: the Core-ier, a pun built around the iconic Cores of Sorcery. "Courier... Core-ier... The Ruby Core-ier." It felt right immediately. Jack brought in his friend SpinScott to help with writing, but the workload was still immense: writing, editing, layout, printing, shipping. He handled the layouts himself through issues zero to nineteen. Then, as he was preparing for issue twenty, a graphic designer found him a
news.title_pt: Além do Feed: A Revista de Fãs de Sorcery em Impressão
news.summary_pt: A criatividade e a paixão pela Sorcery: Contested Realm ganharam vida com a criação da revista The Ruby Core-ier, feita por fãs. Iniciada por Jack/@ElektrickSorcerer após o Courtesan Cup de 2024, a revista busca celebrar o jogo e sua comunidade, trazendo um toque nostálgico e uma qualidade de jornal. Com a ajuda de SpinScott, Jack enfrentou o desafio de criar um fanzine impresso que refletisse a essência de Sorcery.

news.title_en: 2026 Grand Contests: Where the Realm Gathers
news.content_en: Grand Contests are where the realm gathers. Players who have built communities at their local stores, who have contested Cornerstone Champions, or have felt the pull of something larger. This is where they converge. Where Cornerstone Championships celebrate the communities that build them, Grand Contests widen the circle further. These regional gatherings bring players together from across multiple communities, where the creativity, mastery, and wonder that began at local tables meet on a larger stage. The journey that started with a few players at a local store, which grew through Store Kit events and culminated in a Cornerstone Championship, does not end there. At Grand Contests, players who have never shared a table before find themselves contesting the realm together, recognising in each other the same passion that built their own communities back home. A champion will be crowned, but the players who travel to a Grand Contest carry something back with them beyond a result. New stories, new friends, and a sense of just how wide this community has grown. Each Grand Contest Kit includes: These kits are designed to scale with your event. Additional kits can be added so that every community is set up for a great experience. Marta Molina, Alan Pollack, Drew Tucker, Caio Calazans. These cards speak for themselves. Every promo features hand-painted art, a window into the realm you can hold in your hands. Artist Caio Calazans shares a peek into his process with a time-lapse of Cro
news.title_pt: Grand Contests 2026: Onde o Reino se Reúne
news.summary_pt: Os Grand Contests são eventos que reúnem jogadores de diversas comunidades, celebrando a criatividade e a paixão que surgiram em mesas locais. Com campeonatos que ampliam o círculo, esses encontros oferecem uma experiência única, onde novos amigos e histórias são compartilhados. Cada Grand Contest Kit é projetado para escalar com o evento, garantindo que todos tenham uma ótima experiência.

news.title_en: Where Communities Are Celebrated: 2026 Cornerstone Championships
news.content_en: As Store Kit events bring new players together, local Sorcery communities begin to take shape. Rivalries form, new decks appear, stories are shared, and the circle of players grows stronger with each gathering. The kind of community that deserves a moment of recognition. A chance to celebrate the creativity, wonder, and mastery that have taken root at that store. Cornerstone Championships recognize stores that have built a thriving Sorcery scene and invite their players to come together for a special local championship, where bold ideas, practiced skill, and unforgettable moments all take the spotlight. If Store Kits connect circles of players to their Local Game Store, Cornerstone Championships celebrate and strengthen the communities they have built. While Cornerstone Championships are competitive events, with prizes for top placement, in Sorcery competition is always in service of something larger. A champion is crowned, but what endures is another year of games shared, skills tested, and new players drawn into the fold. Your circle of play has widened, and that is what Cornerstones celebrate. Each Cornerstone Kit supports 8 players. Stores will receive a number of kits based on expected player counts, informed by Sorcery Play Network attendance at local events and Cornerstone participation in 2025. Each Cornerstone Kit includes: The 2026 Cornerstone Kit will be available for a limited time until Q3 2026. Cornerstone Championships are something you build toward. Stores tha
news.title_pt: Onde Comunidades São Celebradas: 2026 Cornerstone Championships
news.summary_pt: Os eventos de Store Kit reúnem novos jogadores e ajudam a formar comunidades locais de Sorcery. Rivalidades surgem, novas estratégias são desenvolvidas e o círculo de jogadores se fortalece a cada encontro. Os Cornerstone Championships celebram essas comunidades, proporcionando um campeonato local especial que destaca criatividade e habilidades.

news.title_en: Where Communities Take Root: The Gothic Store Kit
news.content_en: Every journey into Sorcery begins the same way: a table, a few players, and the simple decision to contest the realm. But before long, the desire to widen the circle begins to stir. To meet new Sorcerers, test new deck ideas, and share the game beyond your own table. Sorcery Store Kits help connect those circles. They bring Sorcery into local game stores, where separate playgroups can meet, merge, and grow into something larger. A couple of friends become a few more players, familiar games become challenges to unravel, and new stories emerge from both sides of the table. Here’s what’s inside. The Gothic Store Kit is built to support regular in-store play, the backbone of local Sorcery communities across the realm. Each kit includes alternate-art promotional cards and playmats to reward players for their participation, their contribution to the community, and their achievement at the table. Each Store Kit Includes: Each Store Kit is designed to support eight players across a season of in-store play. If your community is larger, add more kits to ensure every player at your table is covered. There is no fixed schedule. Every community gathers at its own pace, some weekly, some monthly. What matters is that players have a consistent place to meet, experiment, and share their love of the game. Every meet-up is a chance to test new ideas, discover something new about the realm, and come back to the table a little sharper than before. Store Kits are also your connection to Sorcery's
news.title_pt: Onde Comunidades Florescem: O Gothic Store Kit
news.summary_pt: O Gothic Store Kit é projetado para apoiar o jogo regular em lojas, fortalecendo as comunidades locais de Sorcery. Com cartas promocionais de arte alternativa e playmats, o kit recompensa a participação e o envolvimento dos jogadores. Cada kit é ideal para oito jogadores, permitindo que comunidades se reúnam e compartilhem sua paixão pelo jogo.

news.title_en: Where Tables Connect: Sorcery Organized Play 2026
news.content_en: In 2025, we bolstered the Sorcery social play experience with Sorcery Organized Play. More than 1,800 players gathered across 150 Cornerstone events at local game stores around the world, sharing in those Sorcery moments together. Now, in 2026, we’re writing the next chapter. Today, we’re introducing Sorcery Organized Play 2026, a program designed to help stores build thriving communities, connect players across regions, and celebrate the creativity and imagination that make Sorcery unique. But before we talk about the Gothic Store Kit, Cornerstone events, Grand Contests, or the Avatar of the Realm, we need to talk about something simpler. We need to talk about tables. Sorcery begins at the table. Maybe it’s a kitchen table, a folding table at the back of a game store, or the top table at a Grand Contest. While the spirit of the realm is familiar wherever you sit down, every table has its own stories, its own players, and its own moments. Sorcery Organized Play is what happens when those tables connect. It would be easy to build a ladder and focus on a system where the only story that matters is who finished first. But Sorcery was built around a different vision. Sorcery Organized Play 2026 isn’t a climb, it’s an expanding circle. The goal isn’t to filter players through increasingly narrow levels of competition; the goal is to draw a bigger circle around the table, so that more players, more stores, and more communities can share in the experience of Sorcery together. Organi
news.title_pt: Onde as Mesas se Conectam: Sorcery Organized Play 2026
news.summary_pt: Em 2025, o Sorcery Organized Play uniu mais de 1.800 jogadores em 150 eventos Cornerstone ao redor do mundo. Para 2026, o programa visa fortalecer comunidades, conectar jogadores e celebrar a criatividade que torna Sorcery único. O foco não é apenas na competição, mas em expandir a experiência de Sorcery para mais mesas e jogadores.

news.title_en: Where the Bones Lead: CJ’s SorceryCon Necromancer
news.content_en: Just a few weeks ago, SorceryCon, Sorcery’s largest community-organized and community-run event, brought hundreds of players together in Indianapolis for four days of celebration, competition, and connection. With competitive tournaments running alongside casual pods and smaller meetups, artists sketched and signed, and old friends reunited. It was equal parts high-stakes competition and heartfelt celebration, a gathering built by the community, for the community. We caught up with the SorceryCon constructed champion, CJ, a name you might recognize from strong finishes at the Courtesan Cup and SCG CONs and online leagues. But ask CJ about SorceryCon, and the conversation quickly shifts away from trophies. For him, the highlight of the weekend was reconnecting with friends, meeting new players, and laughing through matches, even during a Top 8 draft that had to be muted because the table couldn’t stop joking. As he puts it, “Even if I came in last place, this event would’ve been worth it. The bonds I’ve made through Sorcery are the highlight.” CJ came prepared to SorceryCon with skeletons, movement tricks, and one very clear philosophy: Value wins games. Let’s dive into the deck he brought to SorceryCon. CJ: "I’m CJ! I’ve been a nerd since ’94 and playing Sorcery since ’24. I run a YouTube channel called Old Fashioned Nerds and help run the Sorcerer’s Summit Discord with Bruce. As for highlights… I’ve had a good run. League podiums, SCG wins, multiple SorceryCon Top 8s, and a
news.title_pt: Onde os Ossos Levam: O Necromante de CJ na SorceryCon
news.summary_pt: Recentemente, a SorceryCon reuniu centenas de jogadores em Indianapolis para celebrar e competir. CJ, campeão do torneio construído, destacou a importância das conexões feitas durante o evento, onde o valor das amizades superou os troféus. Ele trouxe um deck focado em esqueleto e filosofia de que valor ganha jogos.

news.content_en: When we first shared Avatar of the Realm, we described it as the kickoff to Organized Play 2026, a flagship gathering of Sorcery. After a lot of planning and honest conversation, we’ve decided to move Avatar of the Realm to Q4 of this year, rather than holding it in the first half of the year. An event like this asks a lot, and the delay allows us to properly ramp up the team to deliver an experience that meets the community’s expectations, secure a venue that reflects the gathering we want to create, and ensure that when we put something like this into the world, it stands as a clear declaration of Sorcery’s values and how we intend to do things moving forward. As with everything we do at Erik’s Curiosa, we’d rather take the time to build it properly than rush it out too soon. Organized Play 2026 is still very much happening. Store programs, the judge initiative, Gen Con, our SCG CON events, as well as numerous community events, will continue to roll out and build momentum throughout the year. The difference is that the Avatar of the Realm is shifting from being the starting bell to the crescendo; a gathering shaped by a year of stories, rivalries, travels, and shared tables. Avatar of the Realm is meant to be Sorcery’s flagship gathering. A space where mastery and creativity sit side by side. Where players, artists, collectors, and judges share the same room, and where the realm feels less like an idea and more like a place. By moving to Q4, we give ourselves, and you, the
news.title_pt: Atualização do Avatar of the Realm
news.summary_pt: O Avatar of the Realm, que seria o início do Organized Play 2026, foi adiado para o Q4 deste ano. Essa decisão visa garantir uma experiência que atenda às expectativas da comunidade, com um evento que reflita os valores de Sorcery. A programação do Organized Play 2026 continua, com eventos e iniciativas ao longo do ano.

news.title_en: Sorcery Awaits: Join us at Gen Con 2026
news.content_en: Sorcery: Contested Realm is returning to Gen Con 2026, from July 30 to August 2, and we’re delighted to be once again bringing the realm to Indianapolis. After an unforgettable showing last year, marked by packed tables, sold-out events, and the debut of Dragonlord, we’re heading back to Gen Con with renewed energy to build on that momentum and continue to focus on what makes Sorcery special: the people who gather around the table. With more than 72,000 attendees passing through Gen Con last year, the energy in the halls was unmatched, and the Sorcery booth and event space sat right at the heart of it all. This summer, we’re thrilled to invite you to join us in Indianapolis for another standout gathering of players, collectors, and friends. Over the years, Gen Con has become a cherished tradition for us. It’s where we hosted our first events after the release of Alpha, and it will always hold a special place in our hearts as a place where the wider gaming community and the Sorcery community come together to experience Sorcery as it was always meant to be played: together. As in years past, our focus for Gen Con 2026 remains on creating space for these shared moments. Whether you’re opening your very first packs or returning with a well-worn spellbook eager to see familiar faces from other events, you can expect opportunities to jump into games, learn to play, and connect with both the community and the team from Erik’s Curiosa. We’re committed to providing space for players t
news.title_pt: Sorcery Aguarda: Junte-se a nós na Gen Con 2026
news.summary_pt: Sorcery: Contested Realm retornará à Gen Con 2026, de 30 de julho a 2 de agosto, em Indianápolis. Após um evento inesquecível no ano passado, estamos animados para reunir jogadores e colecionadores novamente. Venha experimentar Sorcery como sempre foi meant to be played: juntos!

news.title_en: Sorcery: Contested Realm December 2025 Rulebook Update
news.content_en: As Sorcery: Contested Realm continues to grow, we periodically revisit the rulebook to ensure the rules remain clear, consistent, and easy to reference - especially for newer players who we hope will read the rulebook before exploring the Codex. This December rulebook update aligns the rulebook with the changes introduced by Gothic’s release. If you’ve been playing regularly, very little here should feel unfamiliar. Think of this as a light pass by the Realm’s archivists. For anyone new to Sorcery, this updated rulebook will give you a solid foundation to dive into the realm! The Spellbook size has officially been set to 60 cards, reflecting Erik’s original vision for Constructed play, now made possible by the expanded card pool. Several commonly used terms and mechanics have now been formally added to the Sorcery: Contested Realm rulebook glossary for easier reference: If you’ve already been playing with these mechanics, their functionality hasn’t changed. These updates are about clarity and consistency, especially for newer players. A few existing rules have received small wording updates aligned with what’s already in the Codex to ensure new players reading the rulebook also learn the correct rules: This update is intentionally light, just a cleaner, more readable rulebook as we head into the end of the year. As always, we encourage players to use the annotated rulebook to see exactly what’s changed and to keep playing, experimenting, and telling stories across the Realm Y
news.title_pt: Sorcery: Contested Realm Atualização do Livro de Regras de Dezembro de 2025
news.summary_pt: A atualização do livro de regras de dezembro de Sorcery: Contested Realm traz clareza e consistência, alinhando-se às mudanças da versão Gothic. O tamanho do Spellbook foi fixado em 60 cartas, refletindo a visão original de Erik. Novos jogadores encontrarão uma base sólida para explorar o reino, enquanto jogadores experientes notarão pequenas atualizações de redação para maior clareza.

news.title_en: SorceryCon 2026: The Worlds Largest Community Run Sorcery Event Returns
news.content_en: Each year, the Sorcery: Contested Realm community grows not just in size but in spirit, and nowhere is that spirit more visible than at SorceryCon, the entirely community-run celebration of all things Sorcery. In 2026, the tradition continues as SorceryCon comes to Indianapolis from February 19 to 22 with four days of play, art, and camaraderie, all brought to life by passionate fans who build these events from the ground up. Born from the vision of community organizers who simply wanted to gather Sorcery players under one roof, SorceryCon has become a hallmark of the realm: a place where the casual kitchen table energy of the game blends with artists, competitive players, collectors, cosplayers, and newcomers all find a home. “We want SorceryCon to be a space where every kind of fan can share what they love most about the game,” shares Alex West, co-founder of SorceryCon. “Whether that’s competing, trading, creating, or just meeting people from across the world, this is where it all comes together.” Across four days, attendees can expect a full slate of community-run events and activities. While the complete schedule can be found on sorcerycon.com, here’s a glimpse at what’s in store. Two marquee tournaments anchor the weekend: Designed by the community, these headline events welcome players of all skill levels, giving everyone the chance to test their mettle and enjoy a competitive but friendly environment. Between main events, SorceryCon will feature a wide range of format
news.title_pt: SorceryCon 2026: O Maior Evento Comunitário de Sorcery Retorna
news.summary_pt: A comunidade de Sorcery: Contested Realm se reúne em Indianapolis de 19 a 22 de fevereiro de 2026 para a SorceryCon, um evento totalmente organizado por fãs. Com torneios, atividades e a energia de jogadores casuais e competitivos, o evento promete ser um espaço inclusivo para todos os amantes do jogo.

news.title_en: The Path Ahead: SCG CON Atlanta Leads the Way in 2026
news.content_en: With the Las Vegas Crossroads behind us, the 2025 Organized Play season comes to a close. From Gen Con to Gelsenkirchen, Melbourne to Houston, and Baltimore to Las Vegas, each Crossroads stop was shaped by your battles, your artist interactions, and the stories you swapped across the realm. Thank you, Sorcerers, for bringing the realm to life at every stop. As we move into the new year, we’re taking time to reflect on the feedback you’ve shared and lessons learned across our 2025 season. Many of you shared what makes Crossroads special, as well as what could make our larger events even better. We want the Avatar of the Realm event to reflect that learning. With that focus in mind, there will be no additional Crossroads events scheduled for the remainder of the 2025 season. This gives us space to prepare the Avatar of the Realm event properly and to support SCG CON’s growing role in introducing new players to Gothic. This is temporary. Organized Play events will return in 2026, kicking off with our Avatar of the Realm event. We’ll have more to share on that soon. SCG CON gatherings will continue to be a pillar of Sorcery structured play in 2026, and Sorcery will remain present at major events throughout the year. The following events will be focused on helping people explore and enjoy Gothic as a set, including introducing new players to the realm. Sorcery returns to SCG CON Atlanta to open 2026 with a full weekend of Gothic-focused play. Whether you are testing your latest br
news.title_pt: O Caminho à Frente: SCG CON Atlanta Lidera o Caminho em 2026
news.summary_pt: Após o Crossroads de Las Vegas, a temporada de Organized Play de 2025 chega ao fim. SCG CON Atlanta abrirá 2026 com um fim de semana repleto de jogabilidade focada em Gothic, enquanto nos preparamos para o evento Avatar of the Realm. Eventos de Organized Play retornarão em 2026, com foco em introduzir novos jogadores ao reino.

news.content_en: With Gothic ushering in new cards and ways to play, it’s the perfect time to tend the garden a little, trimming back a few overgrown branches and letting some restless wings finally come to rest. Some of these adjustments simply formalize how players have already been playing for months, while others smooth out interactions that drifted a little from their original intent as the realm evolved. In the past, smaller adjustments were handled informally through “treat card as… ” notes on the Curiosa.io FAQ, guidance meant primarily for more competitive-minded players, not those battling it out at the kitchen table. With these changes, we’re moving beyond that approach. The following updates are now official card updates, aimed at improving the Sorcery experience across all types of play. You’ll find them clearly labeled on Curiosa.io with the updated card text prominently displayed. We want to be clear. Card updates in Sorcery are rare, and we approach them with care. Because the game is built on top-down design, where art, flavor, and mechanics all serve a shared vision, these updates aren’t about rewriting a card’s story, but preserving its spirit. It’s no small thing to update an Avatar. They’re central to the realm, anchors of identity that shape the very stories we tell. Typically, we prefer to meet power with counterplay, designing new cards to challenge strong archetypes rather than changing them directly. With Gothic expanding the realm, we’ve already introduced tools to
news.title_pt: Sorcery: Atualizações de Cartas do Contested Realm 2025
news.summary_pt: Com a chegada de Gothic, novas cartas e formas de jogar estão sendo introduzidas em Sorcery. As atualizações visam formalizar como os jogadores já estavam jogando e aprimorar as interações no jogo. Essas mudanças são raras e feitas com cuidado, preservando o espírito das cartas, especialmente dos Avatares, que são centrais para o reino.

news.title_en: Your Path to Adventure Awaits! Sorcery: Contested Realm at SCG CON Baltimore 2025
news.content_en: The event schedule for SCG CON Baltimore, October 24-26,  has been revealed! From the recent Cornerstone event at No Land Beyond to last year’s community-run Courtesan Cup, Baltimore has long proven itself a stronghold of Sorcery.  Now, SCG CON Baltimore carries that legacy forward, giving Sorcerers the chance to chart their own course through the realm as part of a weekend woven into the grand tapestry of SCG CON's premier events. At SCG CON, every player can find a place at the table. Two paths lie before you: Together, these paths weave a weekend of adventure: new journeys begun, old friendships renewed, and stories that will echo long after the final rounds. SCG CON regularly hosts Sorcery artists at their events, and we’re thrilled to announce that Ed Beard Jr., the Dragonlord himself, will be among their ranks at SCG CON Baltimore. In addition, as with our other official events featuring Crossroads tournaments this year, the Dragonlord Mini Set will be available for purchase in limited quantities. More details will be shared in the weeks ahead. Whether you are looking to discover your first deck in a Community Precon event or are looking to compete in the Crossroads Championship, whichever way you choose to explore the realm, SCG Con Baltimore is your chance to experience Sorcery alongside a welcoming and passionate community. Grand Contests bring together players from across regions for Sorcery's largest competitive and community celebrations. Here's what you need to k
news.title_pt: Seu Caminho para a Aventura Aguarda! Sorcery: Contested Realm no SCG CON Baltimore 2025
news.summary_pt: O SCG CON Baltimore, que ocorrerá de 24 a 26 de outubro, promete uma experiência incrível para os fãs de Sorcery. Com a presença de Ed Beard Jr., o Dragonlord, e a venda limitada do Dragonlord Mini Set, os jogadores terão a oportunidade de explorar o reino em eventos como o Community Precon e o Crossroads Championship. Junte-se a uma comunidade apaixonada e viva a aventura!

news.title_en: Gameplay: Gen Con 2025 Crossroads Championship Finals Match
news.content_en: Relive the epic finals of the Gen Con 2025 Crossroads Championship as Nate “Duo” Smith battles Fuenfrappe in a high-stakes Druid vs. Druid showdown. Truly a masterclass in top-level Sorcery: Contested Realm gameplay. Once you’ve enjoyed the battle, don’t miss our deck spotlight from the champion for a full breakdown of the winning list - spoiler warning! Grand Contests bring together players from across regions for Sorcery's largest competitive and community celebrations. Here's what you need to know. Cornerstone Championsihps celebrate the communities you've built, and invite the realm to join you. © 2024 by Erik's Curiosa Limited. All Rights Reserved.
news.title_pt: Gameplay: Finais do Campeonato Crossroads da Gen Con 2025
news.summary_pt: Reviva as épicas finais do Campeonato Crossroads da Gen Con 2025, onde Nate “Duo” Smith enfrenta Fuenfrappe em um duelo de Druida contra Druida. Uma verdadeira aula de gameplay de alto nível em Sorcery: Contested Realm. Não perca o destaque do deck do campeão para uma análise completa da lista vencedora!

news.content_en: The 2025 Sorcery: Contested Realm Organized Play season has officially begun! Running now through the end of the year, Cornerstone events are the heart of local Sorcery communities - a celebration of gameplay, camaraderie, and connection. Hosted by the very stores that serve as the cornerstones of the realm, these events invite new players and returning veterans alike to gather, play, and tell their stories in the realm. This past weekend, the first Cornerstone events kicked off across multiple continents, and the response has been incredible. From new friendships forged over the battlefield to thrilling top-deck victories, these opening events captured the very essence of Sorcery: Contested Realm - tactical depth, imaginative art, and rich community storytelling. “We just loved it! This event was just a great opportunity to spend a day with the warm Sorcery community and have fun battling for the realm!” shared Nicolas, part of a dynamic father-son duo who joined in the fun at Plenty of Games in Melbourne, Australia. Our next Cornerstone event kicks off this weekend at No Land Beyond in Baltimore! If you’re looking to join the fun, it’s easy to find - or register for a Cornertsone event near you! The excitement isn’t limited to local play. While next week's 128-person Gen Con Crossroads event is sold out, there's still time to get involved in Europe’s first Crossroads event taking place September 27–28, 2025, and hosted by the White Rabbit Community Game Store in the Metropo
news.title_pt: Eventos Cornerstone de 2025 Estão em Andamento
news.summary_pt: A temporada de Organized Play de 2025 de Sorcery: Contested Realm começou oficialmente! Os eventos Cornerstone celebram a comunidade local de Sorcery, reunindo novos jogadores e veteranos para jogar e compartilhar histórias. O primeiro evento ocorreu no último fim de semana e foi um grande sucesso, com novas amizades e emocionantes vitórias. O próximo evento será neste fim de semana em No Land Beyond, em Baltimore!
